title: Unique Predecessor Characterization Away from $1$
statement:
Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $x \in P$, $x \neq 1$ if and
only if there exists a unique $u \in P$ such that $S(u)=x$.

current_dependencies.title: Distinguished Element Is Not a Successor
Predecessor Existence and Uniqueness Away from $1$
